text: Advise to give 2nd dose after 8 hours and to give dose twice daily for 2 more days.
All medicines have an expiration date, after which they may not be effective or could be harmful.
Emphasize that it is important to give the amoxicillin for the full 5 days, even if the child feels better.
Counsel caregiver on feeding or refer the child to a supplementary feeding programme, if available.
The caregiver should give the first dose of treatment in your presence.
Advise caregiver to give more fluids and continue feeding.
Advise caregiver, if needed: WHEN and WHERE is the next vaccine to be given?
Advise caregiver on sleeping under a bednet (ITN).
Advise on when to return: go to nearest health facility immediately if child cannot drink or feed, becomes sicker, or has blood in the stool.
Give zinc for the full 10 days, even if the diarrhoea stops.
Follow up child in 3 days.
Follow up child on return at least once a week until child is well.
Explain why the child needs to go to the health facility.
Arrange transportation, and help solve other difficulties in referral.
If any danger sign, REFER URGENTLY to health facility.
Write a referral note.
If RDT is positive, give oral antimalarial AL (Artemether-Lumefantrine). Give twice daily for 3 days.
Give oral antibiotic (amoxicillin tablet - 250mg). Give twice daily for 5 days.
Give ORS. Help caregiver give child ORS solution in front of you until child is no longer thirsty.
Give zinc supplement. Give 1 dose daily for 10 days.
Give rectal artesunate suppository (100 mg) for pre-referral treatment of fever with severe danger signs.
